missing_perspectives.what: East Malaysians bear 1MDB debt burden through federal revenue despite receiving less development spending; 5% petroleum royalty while helping service federal debt for Peninsula megaprojects.
332 under-30 bankruptcies in Jan-Sept 2025 (+60% YoY) while inheriting 1MDB debt burden through 2039.
Specific reform demands not incorporated: political financing bill, GLC governance reform, Jho Low extradition.

alternative_framings.current: 1MDB debt burden primarily as B40 issue on 'single mothers'.
alternative_framings.alternative: Generational transfer — RM24.46B through 2039 will be repaid by young Malaysians now entering workforce.
Patronage is rational response to structural incentives — absence of political financing law creates vacuum forcing unregulated donations.

international_parallels.rule_or_statistic: State Capture Commission report; 1,300+ corruption convictions by 2026; recommendations only partially implemented.
international_parallels.lesson: Conviction of former leader doesn't automatically dismantle state-capture networks.

international_parallels.rule_or_statistic: Danantara sovereign wealth fund launched 2025; CEO cited 1MDB comparisons, emphasized BPK/KPK oversight.
international_parallels.lesson: 1MDB cautionary tale has live ASEAN relevance.

counter_arguments.counter: 37% education-budget comparison may overstate opportunity cost — RM66.2B MOE is gross figure; debt service is fixed obligation.
Current administration has conducted 20 stakeholder engagement sessions and commissioned a public perception study; bill reportedly in drafting.
Government perspective: institutional reforms take time; Madani Accountability Framework includes Ombudsman Bill and FOI legislation; verdict itself demonstrates judicial independence.

missing_facts.fact: RM31.2B recovered 1MDB assets as of Sept 2025 (~70% of estimated RM42B misappropriated).
US DOJ repatriated US$1.2B+ in 1MDB-linked assets; total US recoveries exceed US$1.7B.
Malaysia CPI score unchanged at 50 points in 2024 for 2nd consecutive year (rank 57/180).
KRI Dec 2024 working paper finds B40/M40/T20 outdated; recommends B20/M50/T30.
KK High Court ruling Oct 17 2025: federal govt acted unlawfully on Sabah's 40% net-revenue entitlement; arrears potentially RM150B+.
No clear legislative timeline, no draft bill, no indication of tabling for Political Financing Bill as of April 2026.

gender_gaps.missing: B40 single-mother claim lacks Malaysia gender-disaggregated data on 1MDB fiscal incidence.
Role of women in delivering judiciary — CJ Tengku Maimun, 6 of 11 Federal Court judges women (2025).

economic_gaps.missing: B40/M40/T20 used without acknowledging KRI B20/M50/T30 recommendation.
RM31.2B in recovered assets not accounted for — net fiscal impact ~RM10.8B after recoveries.

geographic_gaps.missing: Peninsular-centric. Sabah/Sarawak contribute oil/gas revenue (5% royalty) while helping service Peninsula-accumulated federal debt.
Oct 2025 KK High Court ruling on Sabah 40% revenue entitlement not mentioned.

overall_assessment: Factually sound on verdict, debt figures, stalled political financing. Materially incomplete: (1) no East Malaysian perspective; (2) omits generational dimension (332 under-30 bankruptcies, debt through 2039); (3) doesn't account for RM31.2B recovered assets; (4) uses contested B40/M40/T20 framework.
